_comment: Hand-curated category schema driving /packages and the home Forge. See docs/plans/2026-05-21-packages-page.md §2. Edit this; the generator never writes here.

editions.title: Anvil
editions.blurb: Base edition — full WorldFoundry stack, retro toolkit, light emulators, game frameworks, image tools. The Distrobox devbox image installs this and nothing else.

editions.title: Sprite
editions.blurb: Anvil + heavy graphics and audio production: Krita, GIMP, Inkscape, MyPaint, mtPaint, GrafX2, Tux Paint, the DAW stack, and trackers.

editions.title: Atelier
editions.blurb: Sprite + everything else: ROM-bundled vintage emulators, heavy console emulators, FOSS engine reimplementations, free games, mobile development, cross-repo dev deps. Around 10 GB on top of bare Ubuntu.

categories.title: World Foundry GDK
categories.blurb: Cross-platform engine and 10 CLI authoring tools — cdpack, iffcomp, iffdump, levcomp, lvldump, oaddump, oas2oad, prep, textile, blender-asset-finder-cli. Plus the engine build-deps (build-essential, cmake, libx11-dev, libgl) via worldfoundry-development.

categories.title: Blender
categories.blurb: Blender preconfigured with the WorldFoundry add-ons (editor exporter + level importer/exporter) and the blender-asset-finder.

categories.title: Retro toolkit
categories.blurb: Retro-porting and reverse-engineering toolchain. MAME, Ghidra, radare2, binwalk, and the 6502 / Z80 / m68k / 6800-family assemblers and disassemblers — the bench for getting old code running again.

categories.title: Emulators
categories.blurb: MAME plus a four-way split — light console (mednafen, fceux, stella), heavy console (dolphin-emu, pcsx2, yuzu, retroarch), vintage home computers (dosbox-x, hatari, fs-uae, openmsx), and multiverse vintage (vice, atari800).

categories.title: Game frameworks
categories.blurb: 2D/3D game-dev frameworks, headers, and shader tools — Tiled, SDL2/3, SFML, Allegro5, libtcod, glslang, spirv-cross, spirv-tools.

categories.title: Image tools
categories.blurb: Raster and vector paint plus pixel-art suites: Krita, GIMP, Inkscape, MyPaint, mtPaint, GrafX2, Tux Paint. Plus the ImageMagick / GraphicsMagick CLIs in Anvil.

categories.title: Audio & trackers
categories.blurb: Audio production stack — Ardour, Audacity, LMMS, Rosegarden, Hydrogen, Qtractor, Mixxx — and the tracker suite (MilkyTracker, Schism, Furnace, openmpt123).

categories.title: Games & reimplementations
categories.blurb: Free games (0 A.D., SuperTuxKart, Widelands, Warzone 2100, OpenTTD, Freeciv, Freedoom, Hedgewars) and FOSS engine reimplementations (VCMI, OpenRCT2, Fheroes2, Exult, Sauerbraten — bring your own data).

categories.title: Mobile development
categories.blurb: Android (OpenJDK 17, adb, NDK r26c) and Linux-side iOS tooling (libimobiledevice, ideviceinstaller, usbmuxd, ifuse).

_forge_home_page_layout._comment: Six cards on the home page, in 3×2 grid. Each card resolves to a `category_slug` (above) at SSR time, pulling its install_command, package count, and headline size from packages-data.json. The card copy fields (title, role, icon) are duplicated from the category for clarity — if they diverge from the category, SSR uses the category as source of truth and warns.
